DESPITE ITS LENGTH OF OVER 1,000 WORDS, THE CHRISTENSEN DECLARATION FAILS TO STATE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE, AS REQUIRED BY CCP 430.41. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS ARE NOW DUE BY SEPTEMBER 6, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS WELLARDINE PUBIEN, AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. Despite its length of over 1,000 words, the Christensen declaration fails to state parties met and conferred in person, by telephone, or by video conference, as required by CCP 430.41. Parties are ordered to comply with the Code. Responsive pleadings are now due by September 6, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
